Were injured in a fall and needed medical (including surgical) treatment. Have experienced a loss of consciousness related to a fall (also see the NICE guideline on transient loss of consciousness ['blackouts'] in over 16s ). Have been unable to get up independently after a fall. Have had 2 or more falls in the last year. See the sections on: comprehensive falls assessment and community settings – comprehensive falls management .

How to move around safely and stay as active as possible while in hospital, and when and how to seek help (for example, if they need to call for assistance to go to the bathroom). How to use unfamiliar equipment during their admission, for example, bed controls and the call bell.
